questions.text: What is the first command given in Surah Al-Ma'idah?
questions.answer: To fulfill all contracts and obligations ('Uqud').

questions.text: Which famous verse signifies the completion of the Islamic religion?
questions.answer: Verse 3: 'This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion'.

questions.text: What is the ruling on the food of the People of the Book (Jews and Christians)?
questions.answer: Their food (specifically slaughtered meat that meets requirements) is lawful (Halal) for Muslims to eat.

questions.text: Describe the incident of the two sons of Adam mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Qabil (Cain) killed Habil (Abel) after Habil's sacrifice was accepted and Qabil's was not, demonstrating the first murder in human history.

questions.text: What moral lesson does Allah derive from the story of the murder of Habil?
questions.answer: That killing one innocent soul is like killing all of humanity, and saving one soul is like saving all of humanity.

questions.text: What did the Children of Israel say to Musa when commanded to enter the Holy Land?
questions.answer: They said, 'Go, you and your Lord, and fight. Indeed, we are remaining right here' (Verse 24).

questions.text: What are the four components of Wudu mentioned in Verse 6?
questions.answer: Washing the face, washing the arms up to the elbows, wiping the head, and washing the feet up to the ankles.

questions.text: What is the final ruling on intoxicants (Khamr) and gambling (Maysir) in this Surah?
questions.answer: They are declared 'Rijs' (abominations) from the handiwork of Satan and are strictly prohibited.

questions.text: Why did the Disciples (Hawariyyun) ask for a 'Table Spread' from heaven?
questions.answer: To eat from it, to put their hearts at rest, and to know that Isa (AS) had told them the truth.

questions.text: On the Day of Judgment, what will Allah ask Isa (AS) regarding his divinity?
questions.answer: Whether he told people to take him and his mother as gods besides Allah; he will deny it, affirming he only told them to worship Allah.
